source.definition: The day after today; on the following day.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'tomorrow' comes from Old English 'to morgenne', meaning 'at morning' or 'on the morrow', originally referring to the next day.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „tomorrow” provine din engleza veche 'to morgenne', care însemna 'dimineața' sau 'în ziua următoare', referindu-se inițial la ziua următoare.

target.definition: Der Tag nach heute; am folgenden Tag.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'mâine' derives from the Latin 'mane', meaning 'early in the morning', and refers to the next day.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „mâine” provine din latinescul 'mane', care înseamnă 'dimineața devreme', desemnând ziua următoare.

examples.sourceText: I will come to visit tomorrow.
Tomorrow the weather will be better.
examples.targetText: Mâine vremea va fi mai bună.

learningTips.text: Think of 'mâine' sounding like 'mine'—imagine the next day belongs to you, 'my day' = 'mâine'.
Remember: 'tomorrow' means the next day, just like 'mâine' in Romanian refers to the following day.
Imagine saying: 'I will come tomorrow (mâine)', meaning the next day that is yours.
Connect 'mâine' to the Latin root 'mane' meaning early morning, just like 'tomorrow' relates to the next morning.
